body_preview:
Gloria,

Just checked the calendar against the new survey dates. Your Fehmarn Belt wreck survey runs September 12-19, and Lars's regional football tournament is the 14th - kickoff in Lübeck at 09:30. He needs to be there by 09:00 at the latest. I can cove
subject: Gloria

body_preview:
Erik,

Thursday return is manageable. The underwater survey wraps field operations Wednesday evening; I'll take the 06:15 ferry from Puttgarden and be home by 19:00. Book Lübeck for you and Lars.

Tell Astrid I'll be at the lesson. Frau Kellner will manag
subject: Re: Erik

body_preview:
Astrid,

The article you forwarded last week - the one about the Jutland Bank survey finds - I had already flagged it from the journal alert, but I appreciated it anyway. The ceramic assemblage they're attributing to a mid-17th-century Dutch fluyt is inte
subject: Astrid

body_preview:
Mama,

The 5th works perfectly, I already checked with work. I'm taking Friday as annual leave so I can come down Thursday evening if that's not too early - I'd rather not arrive at midnight again like last time.

About the article: I figured you'd seen i
subject: Re: Mama

body_preview:
Astrid,

Thursday evening is fine. Come when you can. I'll leave a key with the downstairs neighbor if the seminar runs late.

Lars has a conference in Lübeck that weekend, so it will be just the three of us. The astrolabe photo is striking, you're right
subject: Re: Astrid

body_preview:
Hi Mama,

Quick question before I book the train - are you around the weekend of June 28th? I've been meaning to visit for months and keep letting it slip. I read something about a new Bronze Age anchor find in the Wadden Sea that mentioned Nordmeer Marit
subject: Hi Mama

body_preview:
Lars,

June 28th works. Sunday morning is blocked - I have writing from 9 to 11 at minimum, probably longer if the grant section cooperates. Saturday is clear.

The Wadden Sea anchor is not mine; that's the Kiel group. My current work is the Schlei estuar
subject: Re: Lars

body_preview:
Hi Mama,

Saturday works perfectly. I'll skip the 7am swim - I like you but not at 7am. Dinner Saturday evening?

Also, I'm bringing Mia if that's alright. We've been together about four months now; she's doing her master's in environmental law in Bremen.
subject: Re: Hi Mama

body_preview:
Lars,

Mia is welcome. Dinner Saturday, 7pm - your father is cooking, forewarned.

The conference is in October, not September. Astrid has never once correctly retained a date. It's the Nordic Maritime Heritage Symposium in Bergen; I'm presenting on dendr
subject: Re: Lars

body_preview:
Liebe Mama,

Ich komme Anfang Juli, Wochenende des 5.-6. - falls das bei euch passt. Erik und die Kinder bleiben in Hamburg, also nur ich, was bedeutet, wir haben Zeit zum Reden.

Das Kapitel über die Flensburger Förde-Wracks macht mir gerade Schwierigkei
subject: Liebe Mama

body_preview:
Liebe Gloria,

Der 5. Juli passt sehr gut. Dein Vater freut sich schon - er hat letzte Woche den Garten neu bepflanzt und wartet auf ein Publikum. Ich sage ihm, du kommst wegen der Luft und nicht wegen der Tomaten.

Das mit den Flensburger Daten klingt fr
subject: Re: Liebe Gloria

body_preview:
Liebe Gloria,

I was clearing out the loft here in Kiel this weekend and found a crate I hadn't opened in years. Inside: six logbooks belonging to your grandfather Henning, dated 1953-1968. Fishing routes, North Sea and into the Baltic. Some entries are d
subject: Liebe Gloria

body_preview:
Lieber Papa,

Don't touch them. I mean that specifically - don't repack them, and don't move them somewhere 'drier' without first noting the original storage conditions. Where and how they've been kept matters for the paper dating.

Depth soundings and po
subject: Re: Lieber Papa

body_preview:
Liebe Gloria,

I've left everything exactly where it was. The crate is in the northeast corner of the loft, on top of your grandmother's sewing table. I put a cloth over it so nothing falls on it. I haven't touched the contents.

The bindings look intact
subject: Re: Liebe Gloria

body_preview:
Karin,

The DFG portal is showing a hard deadline of 14 July for the Priority Programme submission. I've completed sections 2 and 3 - fieldwork methodology and the Fehmarn Belt site rationale - but your comparative iconography section is still just a rough stub
subject: Karin

body_preview:
Gloria,

Apologies - the iconography draft has been sitting at 80% for two weeks because the Schleswig finds from last autumn still haven't been formally catalogued. I've been working from my own field photographs, which is fine for argument but not for c
subject: Re: Gloria

body_preview:
Karin,

Thursday evening works. Flag the gaps, I'll sort out which ones are fatal and which ones we can reference as 'forthcoming catalogue, Schleswig-Holstein State Museum, 2027.' Reviewers accept that if you word it precisely.

The Kiel symposium: yes,
subject: Re: Karin

body_preview:
Gloria,

Draft sent - 1,400 words, three gaps flagged in red. The middle one is genuinely thin and I'd rather we acknowledge the limitation directly than paper over it. In my experience DFG reviewers respect honesty about scope more than they used to.

Go
subject: Re: Gloria
